prompt: A Japanese Edo-period Ukiyo-e woodblock print of a massive, ancient, asymmetrical pine tree. The trunk splits into distinct, binary branches, which split again into smaller binary branches, visually representing a decision tree algorithm. Flat, unmodulated areas of muted indigo, ochre, and pale vermillion ink. Strong, continuous dark ink contour outlines around the branches and stylized clouds. Visible woodgrain texture in the negative space. No modern 3D shading, no photorealism, no text.
tradition_approach: Employed flat unmodulated colors, strong dark contour outlines, visible woodgrain textures, and an asymmetrical composition to map the binary splits of a decision tree onto a stylized pine tree.
